rationale: The cited paper is used as a methodological source for measurement in the citing study. In the measure-validation section, the authors explicitly include a single-item satisfaction scale from Mittal and Kamakura (2001) in an auxiliary survey and compare it with ACSI and YouGov measures to establish convergent validity. This is a concrete functional use in the empirical setup, even though the cited paper is not central to the main short-selling hypotheses or mediation theory.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Mittal and Kamakura (2001) as a source of a customer satisfaction measurement item in its validation exercise. It uses that prior scale as a benchmark to show consistency of its focal satisfaction metrics.
